conditions.evidence_text: In the semipreparative LCCC system, which was used in the first dimension, the mobile phase was delivered by JASCO 880 PU pump (Japan Spectrosopic Company, Tokyo, Japan) at a flow rate of 2.0 mL min−1. Samples were injected manually using Rheodyne 7125 injection valve (Rheodyne, Cotati, CA, USA) equipped with a 500 μL loop. A semi-preparative column was used for all measurements: Spherisorb ODS2 (250 mm × 10 mm, 5 μm, 80 Å) from PhaseSep (Deeside, UK). A SICON LCD 201 RI detector was combined with the DDS70. Critical conditions for PBO were realized in 88 wt% THF–water and for PHO in 92 wt% THF–water.
conditions.physical_address: Institute of Chemistry, Karl-Franzens-University, Heinrichstrasse 28, A-8010 Graz, Austria

conditions.evidence_text: In the semipreparative LCCC system, which was used in the first dimension, the mobile phase was delivered by JASCO 880 PU pump (Japan Spectrosopic Company, Tokyo, Japan) at a flow rate of 2.0 mL min−1. Samples were injected manually using Rheodyne 7125 injection valve (Rheodyne, Cotati, CA, USA) equipped with a 500 μL loop. A semi-preparative column was used for all measurements: Spherisorb ODS2 (250 mm × 10 mm, 5 μm, 80 Å) from PhaseSep (Deeside, UK). A SICON LCD 201 RI detector was combined with the DDS70. Critical conditions for PBO were realized in 88 wt% THF–water and for PHO in 92 wt% THF–water.
conditions.physical_address: Institute of Chemistry, Karl-Franzens-University, Heinrichstrasse 28, A-8010 Graz, Austria

conditions.evidence_text: In the analytical LCCC system (second dimension), the mobile phase was delivered by an ISCO 2350 HPLC pump (ISCO, Lincoln, NE, USA) at a flow rate of 0.5 mL min−1. Samples were injected manually using a Rheodyne 7125 injection valve (Rheodyne, Cotati, CA, USA) equipped with a 500 μL loop. A refractive index detector RI 2414 (Waters) or a SEDEX 45 ELSD (Sedere, France) was connected to the DDS 70. Nitrogen was used as carrier gas, and the pressure at the nebulizer was set to 1.0 bar (evaporator temperature: 30°C). Normal phase column used for LCCC in second dimension was Nucleosil 100-5 OH 5 μm, silica-based diol phase; 4.6 mm × 250 mm; particle diameter: 5 μm; pore diameter: 100 Å (Macherey-Nagel, Dueren, Germany). Critical conditions for PEO exist at 75.7 wt% acetone–water.
conditions.physical_address: Institute of Chemistry, Karl-Franzens-University, Heinrichstrasse 28, A-8010 Graz, Austria
